scenario: Vector Healthcare integrates 4000 user tasks per day in Camunda 8.8 with an external case-management UI rather than Tasklist. The team must link each user task to a custom application URL using a Camunda user task implementation, without using the formKey attribute reserved for job worker-based tasks.
question: Which attribute on the zeebe:formDefinition extension element carries the custom external reference for a Camunda user task?

options.text: The externalReference attribute on zeebe:formDefinition, whose value is interpreted by the custom application and is not shown in Tasklist for Camunda user tasks
The formKey attribute on zeebe:formDefinition, which is always the right place for an external URL on both Camunda user tasks and job worker-based user tasks
The formId attribute on zeebe:formDefinition, which can hold either a Camunda Form ID or an external URL depending on the configured bindingType value
The bindingType attribute on zeebe:formDefinition, set to a value such as external so the engine treats the formId as an external custom application reference

a.text: Correct. For Camunda user tasks, a custom form reference is specified using the externalReference attribute on zeebe:formDefinition. The value is interpreted by the implementation and a custom form reference is not shown in Tasklist.

b.text: Incorrect. The formKey attribute is reserved for user tasks with a job worker implementation; Camunda user tasks use the externalReference attribute for custom references.

c.text: Incorrect. The formId attribute identifies a Camunda Form by its ID, not an external URL; bindingType controls which version of that Camunda Form is used.

d.text: Incorrect. The bindingType attribute selects between latest, deployment, and versionTag for Camunda Forms; there is no documented external bindingType that repurposes formId as an external reference.

explanation: For Camunda user tasks, custom form references are placed on the externalReference attribute of the zeebe:formDefinition extension element. The formKey attribute carries custom references only for the job worker implementation of user tasks.
